finding: A Codex-side staging helper spawned git add --pathspec-from-file=- --pathspec-file-nul and waited on stdin, making the user-facing flow look like node atm.mjs git commit was hung even though no commit was running and no files were staged.
expectedBehavior: ATM git commit and git commit-status should fail fast or report a first-class diagnostic when a stdin pathspec git add helper is active, so operators can distinguish a stuck staging helper from a slow governed commit hook.
evidenceOrRepro: On 2026-07-15, process inspection showed git.exe -c core.hooksPath=NUL -c core.fsmonitor= add --pathspec-from-file=- --pathspec-file-nul active while git diff --cached was empty and HEAD remained at 9b963b7.
followUp: Keep commit preflight detection, commit-status diagnostics, and explicit finite auto-stage coverage. ATM auto-stage must continue to use git add -A -f -- <paths>, never stdin pathspec staging; tests/cli/git-commit-task-scoped-staging.test.ts covers the staging command.
